title: Verb ないではおかない
titleEn: Means cannot help but do, will definitely do, or is bound to happen
structure: Verb-ない form + ではおかない
structureEn: Verb-ない form + ではおかない
explanation: Dùng để nói điều gì chắc chắn sẽ khiến một hành động hoặc phản ứng xảy ra; không thể để yên mà không làm. Cấu trúc: Verb-ない form + ではおかない.
explanationEn: The pattern Verb ないではおかない indicates that something definitely occurs as a logical or emotional consequence. It can be translated as cannot not do, can’t help but do, or will inevitably do. The verb is put in the negative form followed by ないではおかない
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ないではすまない
titleEn: Cannot end without doing, must do—an unavoidable moral or social necessity
structure: Verb-ない form + ではすまない
structureEn: Verb-ない form + ではすまない
explanation: Dùng để nói không thể không làm vì trách nhiệm, đạo lý hoặc áp lực xã hội. Có thể hiểu là “buộc phải”, “không làm thì không xong”. Cấu trúc: Verb-ない form + ではすまない.
explanationEn: The grammar Verb ないではすまない expresses that something must be done (often for moral, social, or personal reasons) and cannot be left undone. It implies an obligation or inevitability, akin to I cant get away without doing it
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ないまでも
titleEn: Even if not to the extent of..., although not completely
explanation: Dùng để nói dù chưa đến mức X, nhưng ít nhất cũng đạt mức gần đó hoặc có điều tương tự. Cấu trúc: Verb-ない form + までも.
explanationEn: The pattern ないまでも is used when acknowledging that something doesn’t reach a certain extreme, but at least some part or degree of it applies. Often translated as even if not entirely... or though not as far as
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ないものだろうか
titleEn: Expressing hope or wonder if something can be done: Isnt there a way...?, Cant we...?
structure: Verb-negative (often negative potential) + ものだろうか
structureEn: Verb-negative (often negative potential) + ものだろうか
explanation: Dùng để bày tỏ mong muốn hoặc trăn trở “không có cách nào để... sao?”. Thường dùng khi người nói hy vọng có giải pháp. Cấu trúc: Verb-negative (often negative potential) + ものだろうか.
explanationEn: This grammar point is used when the speaker expresses the hope, wish, or wonder if an action could be possible. It creates the nuance of Isnt there some way...? or Cant I/you/we...? in English, often with the negative potential form
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ないものでもない
titleEn: Expresses the idea that its not entirely impossible or non-existent: Its not that I dont..., Its not that there arent
structure: Verb-negative form (ない form) + ものでもない
structureEn: Verb-negative form (ない form) + ものでもない
explanation: Dùng để nói không phải hoàn toàn không thể hoặc không phải là không có khả năng. Sắc thái dè dặt: “cũng không hẳn là không...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-negative form (ない form) + ものでもない.
explanationEn: The grammar point Verbないものでもない is used to express that a situation is not completely impossible or nonexistent. Literally, it can be translated as its not that there arent... or its not that I never.... It implies that some possibility or partial truth exists
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb の ない Noun
titleEn: Used to modify a noun with a negative verb form; a Noun without (Verb)
structure: Verb in negative form + の + Noun
structureEn: Verb in negative form + の + Noun
explanation: Dùng để bổ nghĩa cho danh từ bằng ý nghĩa “không có hành động/trạng thái X”. Đây là cách nối mệnh đề phủ định với danh từ. Cấu trúc: Verb in negative form + の + Noun.
explanationEn: VerbのないNoun modifies a noun by describing the absence of the verbs action. It indicates a Noun where the action does not happen or a Noun without that action
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ば きりがない
titleEn: Indicates theres no end once you start listing, doing, or saying something: If one starts to..., there will be no end
structure: Verb-conditional form (ば) + きりがない
structureEn: Verb-conditional form (ば) + きりがない
explanation: Dùng để nói nếu bắt đầu làm, nói hoặc liệt kê X thì sẽ không có điểm dừng. Cấu trúc: Verb-conditional form (ば) + きりがない.
explanationEn: The pattern ～ばきりがない is used when expressing that if you start an action—like listing examples or complaining—there is no end. It literally means if (you) do X, theres no limit/end
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb もしないで
explanation: Dùng để phê bình việc thậm chí không làm một hành động tối thiểu. Có thể hiểu là “không buồn làm”, “ngay cả... cũng không”. Cấu trúc: Verb (masu stem) + もしないで.
explanationEn: This pattern is used to express that one has not done (or not even tried to do) some action before proceeding to do something else. It emphasizes doing something without even doing the prior action
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb やしない
titleEn: Expresses a strong denial or refusal: I would never..., No way I would
explanation: Dùng để phủ định mạnh trong văn nói: “chẳng đời nào”, “không hề”. Sắc thái thân mật, đôi khi gay gắt. Cấu trúc: Verb (masu stem) + やしない.
explanationEn: This grammar adds a nuance of strong denial or dismissal of the idea that the speaker (or someone) would do the action. Formed by adding やしない to the verbs stem, it often appears in casual or spoken language
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb よう
titleEn: Expresses the way/manner of doing something or in order to / so that / try to
structure: Verb (dictionary or negative form) + ように (する / etc.)
structureEn: Verb (dictionary or negative form) + ように (する / etc.)
explanation: Dùng để nói cách thức làm một việc hoặc mục đích/định hướng hành động, tùy cấu trúc đi kèm như ようにする. Cấu trúc: Verb (dictionary or negative form) + ように (する / etc.).
explanationEn: This usage of よう most commonly appears as 「Verb辞書形 + ように」. It can mean in order to do something, so that something happens, or in the way someone does something. It is also used in expressions like ～ようにする (to make an effort to) or ～ように言う (to tell someone to do something)
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
